title: Interdisciplinary Discovery Engine Project Brief
abstract: Internal seed brief describing the mission, constraints, and MVP scope of the project.
claims: The project should optimize for testable interdisciplinary hypotheses rather than rhetorical novelty.
The first operational target is a defensible top-5 shortlist with computational validation paths.
mechanisms: rank hypotheses by novelty, feasibility, falsifiability, impact, and validation cost
open_questions: How should the engine promote only defensible hypotheses into the top-5 board?
limitations: Internal planning note rather than external scientific evidence.

title: Agentic Search for Interdisciplinary Hypotheses
abstract: Local methodology note describing KDS architecture, Reason-Act-Observe cycle, and ranked hypothesis framing.
claims: The system should separate acquisition, quality control, semantic core, and synthesis.
Hypothesis output should include evidence trail, novelty framing, and cheap validation routes.
mechanisms: separate acquisition, quality control, semantic core, and synthesis
use semantic distance and cheap validation to prioritize hypotheses
open_questions: Which domain transfers produce the strongest low-cost experimental routes?
limitations: Contains a mix of conceptual framing and unverified external references that require later source-level checking.

title: Biology Domain Seed Note
abstract: Internal note summarizing unresolved biology problems and candidate transfer methods for the MVP.
claims: Aging, gene regulation, regeneration, and noisy high-dimensional measurements remain useful target areas for computational discovery.
Biology can benefit from imported methods when mechanisms are partially known but data is abundant.
mechanisms: network structure in gene and protein interaction systems
open_questions: Which residual structure in biological data reflects control rather than noise?
Which under-characterized biological components can be inferred from network structure?
limitations: Planning note only; not a substitute for literature review.

title: Materials Science Domain Seed Note
abstract: Internal note summarizing unresolved materials-science bottlenecks and candidate transfer methods for the MVP.
claims: Multiscale modeling, phase stability, defect prediction, and rapid design-space screening are promising computational targets.
Materials problems often have rich structured data but expensive direct validation.
open_questions: Which descriptors carry the minimum information needed to preserve target properties?
Which low-cost models best approximate expensive simulations?
limitations: Planning note only; not a substitute for materials-specific prior-art review.

title: Control And Information Theory Domain Seed Note
abstract: Internal note summarizing unresolved control-theory and information-theory bottlenecks and candidate transfer methods for the MVP.
claims: Feedback design, filtering, compression, and robust control provide strong transferable abstractions for other sciences.
Many hard control settings involve uncertainty, delay, partial observability, or limited representations.
open_questions: Which systems outside engineering can be reframed as control problems with measurable residuals?
Which scientific datasets benefit from compressed but mechanistically useful representations?
limitations: Planning note only; not a substitute for formal control-theory sourcing.
